summary: Cally v2 Phase 1 backend test suite: 33/33 tests passed covering BullMQ worker + Redis, Google Calendar webhook, Organizations/Teams/Invitations CRUD + RBAC, Stripe 503-by-design, AI chat error paths, Phase 1 page rendering (/organizations, /ai), Phase 0 regression, and compat shim (/api/user/profile). Worker successfully consumes a live renew-channel job enqueued via the BullMQ Queue API within ~1s. Middleware now correctly passes /api/* through so unauth API calls return clean JSON 401 (previous iteration's complaint is resolved).

minor.issue: Response body is the raw Organization object (no top-level `organization` key wrapping). Spec wording says 'creates Organization' which matches, but downstream tests need to handle either shape. Recommend wrapping in { organization } for consistency with invitation endpoint which returns { invitation, acceptUrl }.

Does not verify the X-Goog-Channel-Token against account.webhookToken — only looks up by channelId. A malicious actor who guesses/learns a channelId (non-secret) could trigger delta-sync spam. Recommend validating channel token equals stored token before enqueueing.
No dedup check — submitting the same email twice creates two pending invitations. Also no email dispatch wiring (acceptUrl is returned to the caller only). Acceptable for MVP but flag for UX.

action_items: (Optional) Wrap POST /api/organizations response in { organization } for shape consistency with invitations/teams endpoints.
(Security) Validate X-Goog-Channel-Token against stored account.webhookToken in /api/integrations/google/webhook before enqueueing sync jobs.
(UX) Dedupe pending invitations per (orgId,email) and wire an actual email send (currently acceptUrl is only returned to the API caller).
critical_code_review_comments: organizations POST: slug uniqueness loop is sequential await inside a while — under high concurrency two requests could both resolve to the same finalSlug before either writes. A unique DB index on slug will guard this but could produce a 500 on the losing side; catch Prisma P2002 and retry.
invitations: HMAC signature is truncated to 24 hex chars (96 bits) — acceptable, but verify the accept route validates the hmac constant-time (timingSafeEqual) rather than === to avoid timing leaks.

stripe/webhook: uses AuditLog as an idempotency store — this is fine, but add a unique index on (action, entityId) to protect against the race where two webhook deliveries race the findFirst+create window.
worker logs show only 'calendar-sync renew-channel id= <n>' without the actual job payload (e.g., accountId). Include accountId in log for traceability during incidents.
ai/chat: returns 400 JSON for 'no credential' but returns plain-text 'Unauthorized' body for 401. Make both JSON for API consistency.
proxy.ts now passes /api/* through — good. Consider also emitting 'WWW-Authenticate: Bearer' on 401 responses for standards compliance.
test_credentials: Each run creates owner_<rand>@cally.local and member_<rand>@cally.local with TestPass123. Existing admin@cally.local / TestPass123 is preserved.
seed_data_creation: Each run creates 2 users, 2-3 Organizations, 1 Team, 1+ Invitations, 1 OpenAI AI credential. All rows remain in Postgres (no cleanup); data is harmless test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Phase 1 tests live at /app/backend/tests/test_cally_phase1.py. Phase 0 tests at /app/backend/tests/test_cally_backend.py. Run with `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/ -v`. The worker-enqueue test writes /app/.enqueue_test_<marker>.cjs and deletes it after (needs to be inside /app so node resolves bullmq/ioredis). Middleware (proxy.ts) now correctly lets /api/* through to route handlers — API endpoints return JSON 401 (not 307 to /login) for unauth. Stripe tests assert 503-by-design (STRIPE_SECRET_KEY blank in .env). If STRIPE_SECRET_KEY is later populated, TestStripe will fail and must be updated to check 400 (validation error) instead.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no functional failures. One test-infra issue encountered and fixed: the BullMQ enqueue snippet needed to run from /app (not /tmp) so Node could resolve bullmq/ioredis from /app/node_modules.
